title: Evaluation Datasets, Hard Negatives, Leakage, and Experiment Design · Transfer Build
summary: An independent team receives a different corpus, query mix, relevance policy, language set, access model, latency budget, harm profile, and evidence duty and transfers evaluation datasets, hard negatives, leakage, and experiment design into a new versioned retrieval and answer test collection. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of versioned retrieval and answer test collection with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Sample real and designed queries across intents, languages, difficulty, freshness, answerability, access, source types, and harm with documented weights.
Create relevance and claim-support judgments with rubrics, independent assessors, adjudication, agreement, and provenance.
Include lexical, semantic, stale, conflicting, unauthorized, near-duplicate, adversarial, and counterfactual hard negatives.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Query Set Sampling
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab RAG engineering team handles case 4406 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to build versioned judgments with hard negatives, slice coverage, agreement, and leakage control; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Eval Leakage Versioning
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-eval-leakage-versioning-oxtrtd" that uses case 3176 to build versioned judgments with hard negatives, slice coverage, agreement, and leakage control. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Eval Leakage Versioning.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Eval Leakage Versioning into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Eval Leakage Versioning, keep Rag Eval Leakage Versioning connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-eval-leakage-versioning-oxtrtd
# Competency: rag-eval-leakage-versioning.
# Case 3176: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data.
def evidence_rag_eval_leakage_versioning_oxtrtd(queries: int = 120, assessors: int = 2, hard_negatives: int = 36, leaked_queries: int = 0, agreement: float = 0.78, slices_covered: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_oxtrtd = "oxtrtd-dtrtxo-3176"
    assert evidence_variant_oxtrtd
    evidence_axes_oxtrtd = (
        "corpusoxtrtd",
        "querydtrtxo",
        "tenant3176",
        "indexoxtrtd3176",
        "promptdtrtxo3176",
        "evaluation3176oxtrtd",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_oxtrtd)) == 6
    if queries <= 0 or assessors < 1 or hard_negatives < 0:
        return False, queries, "dataset-shape"
    if leaked_queries > 0:
        return False, leaked_queries, "evaluation-leakage"
    if agreement < 0.0 or agreement > 1.0:
        return False, 0, "agreement-range"
    if assessors < 2 or slices_covered < 4:
        return False, slices_covered, "judgment-coverage"
    assert hard_negatives < queries
    return True, queries, "versioned-eval-set"
